summary: Matched most of src/melee/it/items/itlizardon.c by replacing many placeholder stubs with concrete item behavior, accessory/effect spawning, projectile spawning, physics/collision wrappers, and hitlag callbacks. The PR also added Lizardon-specific item variable and attribute structs, wired the lizardon item vars into Item.xDD4_itemVar, and tightened itlizardon.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect return types to typed Item_GObj-based signatures.

key_files.role: Main matched implementation for Lizardon item logic, replacing many placeholder markers with C functions.
Added itLizardon_ItemVars and itLizardonAttributes layouts used by the matched implementation.
Updated function prototypes with concrete return types and parameters.
Added lizardon member to the Item.xDD4_itemVar union.

what_changed: Implemented it_802CB8AC initialization logic, clearing itcmd vars, initializing lizardon item vars, calling it_802CC0EC, setting item lifetime/attribute data through it_80279CDC, and playing sound/effect cue via Item_8026AE84.
Converted it_802CB93C from an untyped empty function to void it_802CB93C(Item_GObj* gobj).
Changed it_802CB960 and it_802CB994 from s32-style behavior to void functions; it_802CB960 now calls it_80273454 then it_802CB994 without returning a value.
Implemented motion callbacks for UnkMotion1, UnkMotion2, and UnkMotion3, including animation transitions, physics reuse, collision handling for air/ground states, and false/true return behavior.
Added helper inline routines it_802CBAA8_inline and it_802CBD24_inline to reset lizardon.x70 using attrs->x28 + HSD_Randi(attrs->x2C - attrs->x28).
Implemented it_802CBAA8 to transition to animation state 2, initialize lizardon timers from attributes, set hitlag callbacks to efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll, and set on_accessory to it_802CBD24.

Implemented it_802CBD24 accessory logic using dynamic bone table positions, lizardon timer state, facing toggles, it_8027ADEC, efSync_Spawn for effect IDs 0x453 through 0x456, and calls to it_802CC1CC for random item spawning.
Implemented it_802CBFE4 and it_802CC0EC as state setup functions that call Item_80268E5C and assign hitlag callbacks.

Implemented it_802CC1CC to construct a SpawnItem from bone position/current item position, randomize an angle using attrs->x8/xC, apply velocity using sinf/cosf and facing direction, spawn via Item_80268B18, and conditionally play one of sounds 0x2716-0x2718.
Implemented spawned logic for it_2725_Logic34_Spawned through it_2725_Logic37_Spawned, each spawning a distinct effect ID 0x44E-0x451 scaled by item scale then calling it_802CC5D4.
Implemented it_802CC5D4 item setup with it_80275158, it_80274740, Item_80268E5C, hitlag callbacks, and it_8026B3A8.
Implemented it_802CC684 as velocity damping/scaling using attrs->x4.
Added itLizardon_ItemVars with fields at offsets x60, x64, x68, x6C, x70, and x74[4], preserving the ip+DD4 offset relationship with a pad[0x60].
Added itLizardonAttributes with float fields x0-x10, s32 timer x14, s32 array x18[4], and random range fields x28/x2C.
Added the lizardon member to the Item item-var union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Included newly needed headers for effects, item APIs, bone position helpers, math, and random: ef/eflib.h, ef/efsync.h, it/item.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, MSL/math.h, and sysdolphin/baselib/random.h.
smart_moves: Used a dedicated itLizardon_ItemVars struct rather than continuing to access raw xDD4 offsets, making the matched item-state logic searchable and reusable.
Kept repeated timer-reset logic in small inline helpers, likely to preserve codegen while avoiding duplicate source-level expressions.
Reused existing motion helper functions: UnkMotion2_Phys delegates to UnkMotion1_Phys, UnkMotion2_Coll delegates to UnkMotion1_Coll, and UnkMotion3 follows the same reuse pattern.
Matched hitlag behavior consistently by assigning entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll in each relevant item state setup.
Used scoped temporary blocks around decrement-and-test timer code in it_802CBD24, which can help preserve local variable lifetimes/order for matching.
Preserved an unrolled sequence for x74[0..3] timers and effect IDs rather than forcing a loop that did not compile to the target assembly.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in functions where stack shape mattered, including itLizardon_UnkMotion2_Anim, it_802CBD24, and spawned logic functions.

For attrs->x4 initialization into lizardon.x60, used an explicit bit reinterpretation expression `*(s32*) (void*) (&attrs->x4)`, indicating the match required integer bit-pattern treatment rather than normal float conversion.
decomp_lessons: When item state variables live deep inside Item.xDD4_itemVar, adding an item-specific struct plus a union member can unlock much cleaner matching and future review.
Do not assume repeated adjacent timer/effect cases should be expressed as a loop; the compiler may not auto-unroll it into the target form.
For item setup states, check whether hitlag callback assignments are part of every animation-state transition, not only initial spawn.
Accessory callbacks can be central to item matching: they often combine itcmd vars, per-item timers, bone positions, effect spawns, and child item spawns.
Randomized behavior may require preserving exact expressions such as `attrs->x28 + HSD_Randi(attrs->x2C - attrs->x28)` and angle calculations using the project’s float constants.
Prototype cleanup is part of matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and incorrect return types prevents later functions from being forced into bad casts or fake returns.
Ground/air collision wrappers for items often use the pattern `if ground_or_air == GA_Air then it_8026E15C else it_8026D62C`, with a callback such as it_802CB93C.
SpawnItem construction should preserve field order and parent references: this PR uses owner as x0_parent_gobj and the current item gobj as x4_parent_gobj2.
Stack padding macros may be required even after logic is understood; record where PAD_STACK was needed because those spots can guide future adjacent matches.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variable structs follow the form `itLizardon_ItemVars` and are added as lowercase union members such as `lizardon` under `ip->xDD4_itemVar`.
Item attribute structs follow the form `itLizardonAttributes` and are accessed through `ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`.
Unknown motion callbacks keep the item name and motion role, e.g. `itLizardon_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itLizardon_UnkMotion1_Phys`, and `itLizardon_UnkMotion1_Coll`.
Address-based unknown functions remain named by address, e.g. `it_802CB8AC`, `it_802CBD24`, and `it_802CC1CC`, while their prototypes are still made concrete.
Inline helper names used the callee address plus `_inline`, e.g. `it_802CBAA8_inline` and `it_802CBD24_inline`, to document codegen-sensitive extracted expressions.
Function parameters in cleaned prototypes use `Item_GObj* gobj` where the function operates on an item object, replacing generic `arg0` and `UNK_PARAMS`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Left a repeated four-case decrement/effect-spawn block unrolled because a source loop did not automatically unroll to the target assembly.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(4)`, and `PAD_STACK(16)` in specific functions to match stack layout.
Used static inline helpers for repeated random timer assignment, likely to influence inlining/codegen while preserving readable source.
Used explicit float math through `cosf`, `sinf`, and the constant `0.017453292f` for degree-to-radian conversion in projectile velocity calculation.

Used a bit reinterpret cast from `attrs->x4` to `s32` when initializing `ip->xDD4_itemVar.lizardon.x60`, suggesting the target stores the raw float bit pattern or requires matching load/store behavior.
Used temporary variables and scoped blocks for decrementing timers before branch checks, e.g. `s32 v = timer - 1; timer = v; if (v < 0) ...`, to match instruction ordering.
Adjusted return types from `s32` to `void` where the implementation did not produce a meaningful return, avoiding fake returns in matched code.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.comment: this really smells like an unrolled loop, but when I wrote the loop it wasn't automatically unrolled, so I left it as-is
review_feedback.lesson: Prefer source that matches target codegen over a cleaner loop when the compiler will not reproduce the expected unrolled assembly.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itLizardonAttributes fields x0-x2C to semantic names.
Search for other item PRs that add Item.xDD4_itemVar union members with a padded item-specific struct.
Search for PRs matching accessory callbacks that spawn effects from xBBC_dynamicBoneTable bones.
Compare other Pokemon item matches that use efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll in state setup.
Find other review comments mentioning unrolled loops not generated by the compiler.
